summary: Completed comprehensive testing of MANO Banking Integration, ML Fraud Detection, and PWA features (Iteration 7). Backend: 25/25 tests passed (100%). Frontend: All new components working correctly - BankingDashboard, ThreatAnalyzer with ML, Dashboard tabs navigation.

minor.issue: ML pattern detection threshold (50) may be too high for some obvious fraud patterns. Some messages with clear fraud indicators (prize scams, personal data requests) score below 50 and are not flagged as threats.

issues: Minor React warning: 'Cannot update a component while rendering a different component' in BrowserRouter/Login - does not affect functionality

passed_tests: GET /api/banking/supported-banks - Returns list of 8 Spanish banks
GET /api/banking/summary - Returns comprehensive banking summary with stats
GET /api/banking/transactions - Returns transaction list with risk scores
POST /api/banking/analyze-transaction - High risk transaction flagged with risk factors
Banking endpoints require authentication (401 without token)
POST /api/ml/analyze-text - Detects phishing SMS with URL shortener
POST /api/ml/analyze-text - Safe content returns low risk
POST /api/ml/analyze-text - Detects URL phishing patterns (IP address)
ML detects personal data request patterns
PWA manifest.json accessible and properly configured
Frontend: Dashboard loads with 3 tabs (Analizador IA, Banca Segura, Historial)
Frontend: ThreatAnalyzer component analyzes content and shows results
Frontend: BankingDashboard shows connected accounts and transactions
action_items: OPTIONAL: Consider lowering ML threat detection threshold from 50 to 40 for better fraud detection sensitivity
OPTIONAL: Fix React warning about setState during render in Login component
critical_code_review_comments: Banking service is properly modularized in /services/banking_service.py with simulated Open Banking integration
ML fraud detection in /services/fraud_detection.py correctly implements pattern matching and risk scoring
Threat analyzer in /services/threat_analyzer.py has hybrid LLM+ML approach with proper fallback
PWA manifest.json has all required fields for installable PWA (icons, display, theme_color)
Service worker sw.js implements offline caching, push notifications, and background sync
All new endpoints have proper authentication checks using require_auth decorator
BankingDashboard component properly displays accounts, transactions, and stats
ThreatAnalyzer component shows detailed analysis results with risk score visualization
seed_data_creation: Bank accounts and transactions created during testing via /api/banking/connect endpoint

mocked_apis.banking_api: Banking integration is SIMULATED - does not connect to real banks (Open Banking). Accounts and transactions are generated for demo purposes.
mocked_apis.llm_analysis: Uses ML patterns when EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is not configured or when ML score is high enough (>=80)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Banking and ML features fully tested: Banking endpoints (supported-banks, connect, accounts, summary, transactions, analyze-transaction), ML endpoints (analyze-text, risk-summary, behavior-profile). Dashboard has 3 tabs: Analizador IA (ThreatAnalyzer component), Banca Segura (BankingDashboard component), Historial (threat history). PWA is configured with manifest.json and sw.js. All data-testid attributes are in place for testing.
